heading: Definitions
label: 3058f Definitions
notes: Amendments2020—Par. (6). inserted “of any age” after “individual”.Pub. L. 116–1312016—Par. (6). struck out “older” before “individual”.Pub. L. 114–144
Effective DateSection inapplicable with respect to fiscal year 1992, see , set out as an Effective Date of 1992 Amendment note under .section 905(b)(6) of Pub. L. 102–375section 3001 of this title
amendments: Amendments2020—Par. (6). inserted “of any age” after “individual”.Pub. L. 116–1312016—Par. (6). struck out “older” before “individual”.Pub. L. 114–144
Effective DateSection inapplicable with respect to fiscal year 1992, see , set out as an Effective Date of 1992 Amendment note under .section 905(b)(6) of Pub. L. 102–375section 3001 of this title

children.heading: Office
children.label: 1 Office
children.text: The term “Office” means the office established in .section 3058g(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Ombudsman
children.label: 2 Ombudsman
children.text: The term “Ombudsman” means the individual described in .section 3058g(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Local Ombudsman entity
children.label: 3 Local Ombudsman entity
children.text: The term “local Ombudsman entity” means an entity designated under to carry out the duties described in with respect to a planning and service area or other substate area.section 3058g(a)(5)(A) of this titlesection 3058g(a)(5)(B) of this title

children.heading: Program
children.label: 4 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman program established in .section 3058g(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Representative
children.label: 5 Representative
children.text: The term “representative” includes an employee or volunteer who represents an entity designated under and who is individually designated by the Ombudsman.section 3058g(a)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Resident
children.label: 6 Resident
children.text: The term “resident” means an individual of any age who resides in a long-term care facility.
